climate.description: Classic Yorkshire summer - expect all four seasons. Windy moorland summits amplify conditions.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 76.

course_description.character: Brutal Yorkshire Dales epic featuring seven monster climbs through wild moorland, remote valleys, and iconic high passes. Cinematic scenery meets relentless gradient.
course_description.signature_challenge: Coal Road to Dent Station - relentless 7.8% gradient delivering you to England's highest mainline railway station through remote gritstone wilderness.
suffering_zones: Langcliffe Scar: 2.3km @ 7.8% avg (18% max) - brutal race opener after 3km warm-up
Fleet Moss: Highest road in Yorkshire - bleak moorland exposure
Tan Hill Inn: Highest pub in England - remote grit test
Buttertubs Pass: Iconic Tour de Yorkshire climb with butter churn landmarks
Coal Road: 2.8km @ 7.8% avg to highest mainline station in England
Grinton Moor: 3.8km grind across wild Swaledale moorland

logistics.lodging_strategy: Settle offers B&Bs, pubs, and camping. Grassington (15min drive) and Skipton (20min) provide more options.
logistics.transport: New HQ at Settle with parking, changing rooms, post-ride food and beer. Multiple feed stations including Moorcock Inn.

history.origin_story: Established as Fred Whitton Challenge alternative. Supports Dave Rayner Fund for young cyclists. Iconic UK sportive status.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ultimate Yorkshire Dales sufferfest - 10,500ft of cinematic climbing punishment.

citations.label: Etape du Dales by the Rayner Foundation
